# desec_domain (Resource)

Manages a deSEC DNS zone (domain). Domain names are immutable after creation.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Create a DNS zone for example.com
resource "desec_domain" "example" {
  name = "example.com"
}

# Output the DNSSEC DS records for delegation at your registrar
output "ds_records" {
  value = [for key in desec_domain.example.keys : key.ds if key.managed]
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) The domain name (e.g. `example.com`). Must be unique and is immutable after creation.

### Read-Only

- `created` (String) Timestamp of domain creation in ISO 8601 format.
- `id` (String) The domain name, used as the resource identifier.
- `keys` (Attributes List) DNSSEC public key information for the domain. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--keys))
- `minimum_ttl` (Number) The minimum TTL (in seconds) that can be used for RRsets in this domain. Set automatically by the server.
- `published` (String) Timestamp of when the domain's DNS records were last published.
- `touched` (String) Timestamp of when the domain's DNS records were last touched.

<a id="nestedatt--keys"></a>
### Nested Schema for `keys`

Read-Only:

- `dnskey` (String) The DNSKEY record content.
- `ds` (List of String) The DS record contents (for delegation).
- `managed` (Boolean) Whether this key is managed by deSEC.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

In Terraform v1.12.0 and later, the [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) can be used with the `identity` attribute, for example:

```terraform
import {
  to = desec_domain.example
  identity = {
    name = "example.com"
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
### Identity Schema

#### Required

- `name` (String) The domain name.

In Terraform v1.5.0 and later, the [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) can be used with the `id` attribute, for example:

```terraform
import {
  to = desec_domain.example
  id = "example.com"
}
```

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import desec_domain.example example.com
```
